toxicity.summary_warning: Not to be ingested or touched; all parts are highly toxic and can lead to severe poisoning.

gastrointestinal.name: Violent Vomiting
gastrointestinal.description: Vomiting that can persist after ingestion.

cardiovascular.name: Irregular Pulse
cardiovascular.description: Heart rhythm abnormalities or fluttering sensations.

cardiovascular.name: Hypotension
cardiovascular.description: Dangerously low blood pressure causing fainting.

toxic_parts.description: Contain high concentrations of digitoxin, digoxin, and gitaloxin.
Contain concentrated digitalin and other active glycosides.
Contain significant amounts of gitoxin, digitoxin, and toxic saponins.

handling_safety: Wear protective clothing to avoid skin contact.
Avoid touching face while handling the plant.
Wash hands and forearms thoroughly after contact.
Do not burn any part of the plant.

commonly_mistaken_for.name: Delphinium
commonly_mistaken_for.description: Has spurred flowers rather than foxglove's spotted bell-shaped flowers.

commonly_mistaken_for.name: Penstemon
commonly_mistaken_for.description: Has smaller tubular flowers arranged in airy clusters.

commonly_mistaken_for.name: Lupine
commonly_mistaken_for.description: Has pea-like flowers forming a pointed cone.

commonly_mistaken_for.name: Comfrey
commonly_mistaken_for.description: Has small bell-shaped flowers but lacks foxglove's tall spike.

medicinal_uses.description: Digoxin derived from foxglove may be prescribed for certain heart failure patients.
Used medically for controlling heart rate in some patients.

features_detected.shape: Tubular, elongated bell-shaped flowers hanging downward.
features_detected.color: Pink, lavender, purple, white, cream, or peach flowers.
features_detected.stem: Tall erect hairy stem reaching approximately 3 to 6 feet.
features_detected.arrangement: Flowers arranged on one side of a tall unbranched spike.
features_detected.interior_speckles: Dark purple or burgundy spotted patterns inside flowers.
features_detected.leaves: Crinkled, textured leaves covered with soft hairs and serrated edges.

feature_images.caption: Crinkled, velvety leaves with serrated edges

contact: Wash skin immediately with soap and water.
ingestion: Do not induce vomiting unless instructed.
Clean mouth with a wet cloth.
pets: Remove plant pieces carefully if possible.
